data.title: Tech sector pulls STOXX 600 lower
data.description:
Key points:
  • STOXX 600 down 0.9%
  • Tech lags after ASML results, US chip curbs
  • Defensive sectors lead
  • Wall St futures fall

TECH SECTOR PULLS STOXX 600 LOWER

A decline in European technology shares is dragging the STOXX 600 lower in early trade on Wednesday, after results from ASML and after Nvidia disclosed that the U.S. has imposed curbs on some chip exports to China.

ASML , the world's largest supplier of computer chip-making equipment, warned that tariffs are clouding the outlook, but maintained its annual guidance. Shares are still off 5.4% as net bookings missed estimates in the first quarter.

The tech sector (.SX8P) is Europe's laggard, down 2.7%, dragging down the broader market.

Defensive sectors are outperforming, with utilities (.SX6P), food and beverages (.SX3P) and telecoms (.SXKP) all in positive territory.

The pan-continental STOXX 600 is lower by 0.9%. Britain's FTSE CURRENCYCOM:UK100 is down 0.5%, while France's CAC and Germany's DAX are down 0.8% and 0.9%, respectively.

Looking ahead, markets are now awaiting a speech from Fed Chair Jerome Powell and updates on trade negotiations between the U.S. and major trade partners, with Japan set to kick off talks on Wednesday.

Here's your opening snapshot:

(Samuel Indyk)

*****

WEDNESDAY'S OTHER LIVE MARKETS POSTS:

EUROPE BEFORE THE BELL: FUTURES FALL AS TRADE TENSIONS TAKE ANOTHER TURN CLICK HERE

MARKETS WOBBLE OVER US-CHINA WRANGLING CLICK HERE

data.title: Spot Gold Briefly Rises Above $3,300/oz Level for First Time, ICE Data Shows
Spain Must Put More Cash Into Defense, Bessent Says
data.description:
By Joshua Kirby

Spain must spend more on defense as part of a trade deal with the U.S., Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent told his Spanish counterpart in a meeting.

"Secretary Bessent highlighted the need for greater defense spending by Spain in the context of NATO," the Treasury Department said in a readout after Bessent met Spanish finance & trade minister Carlos Cuerpo in Washington on Tuesday.

President Trump and many of his officials have repeatedly criticized European governments for not contributing enough money to their own defense or to the North Atlantic Treaty Organization military alliance.

Spain is committed to spending more on defense, with military expenditure set to rise to 2% of economic output, Cuerpo told reporters following the meeting. "We will do so in a timely manner," he said. "We will meet that target."

He added that Bessent seemed open to reaching a trade deal.

"Secretary Bessent has sent a very clear message that they want to come to a deal with their main trading partners, including, naturally, the European Union," Cuerpo said.

Still, Spain has some way to go to get defense spending up to levels demanded by Washington, a task complicated by its government's hobbled legislative power in a divided parliament. Spain would have to devote around 8.8% of tax revenue to raise spending from the current level of 1.3% of gross domestic product, analysts at the Scope ratings agency calculated earlier this year.

Meanwhile, Bessent, underlined U.S. opposition to taxes on digital services levied by Spain and other European countries, the department said.

Write to Joshua Kirby at joshua.kirby@wsj.com; @joshualeokirby

data.title: Heineken Delivers Positive Performance in Key Growth MarketsMarket Talk
data.description: Heineken delivered another quarter of positive signs in key growth engines such as Vietnam, Mexico, Brazil and India, Jefferies analysts Edward Mundy and Andrei Andon-Ionita say in a note. The Dutch brewer is currently at an early stage of a multi-year turnaround program made to deliver profitable growth and improving free cash flow, they say. Heineken backed its guidance for 2025 for growth in both volume and revenue, and net profit growth in line with operating profit growth. On the negative side, Heineken unperformed in Europe, but this is likely to improve into the second quarter and face easier comparatives in the third quarter, they add. "As confidence in Heineken's compounding potential increases, shares should re-rate," the analysts add. Shares are up 2.1% at 76.74 euros.(michael.susin@wsj.com)

data.title: Medical device maker Belluscura expects 20% tariff on products sold in US
data.description:
Medical device maker Belluscura on Wednesday said that its products sold in the United States could face levies of up to 20% and that the company was evaluating options to cut down on tariff and production costs in the long term.

Belluscura, which focuses on developing portable oxygen technology and is headquartered in the U.S., also announced that its finance chief, Simon Neicheril, had resigned, with Chairman Paul Tuson set to take on responsibility for finance.

An uncertain economic outlook, made worse by turmoil due to proposed U.S. tariffs, has muted growth prospects for companies globally as they adapt to changing policies and sentiment.

The company last week had withdrawn its outlook for 2025, after warning that a "significant proportion" of the raw materials and parts used in portable oxygen concentrators and other devices are currently made in China, which has been singled out by the U.S. in its trade war.

However, London-listed Belluscura on Wednesday said that its prospects for the second quarter were "encouraging" and that it was considering steps such as manufacturing and sourcing components from other regions to mitigate costs.

The company's shares have fallen nearly 50% since the warning on April 8, taking the stock's losses for the year to over 90%.

data.title: FTSE 100 Snaps 4-Day Winning Streak
data.description:
The FTSE 100 fell over 0.5% on Wednesday, snapping a four-day winning streak as global markets dropped on renewed trade tensions, including fresh US restrictions on Nvidia chip exports to China.

Shares in Bunzl plunged a record 26% after it cut its outlook, citing tougher economic conditions.

Meanwhile, WH Smith missed revenue expectations, while Barratt Redrow reaffirmed homebuilding targets.

Mitie rose after raising profit guidance and announcing a £125 million share buyback.

On the data front, UK inflation eased again, offering brief relief before April's expected jump in bills.

data.title: US Futures Lower, Nvidia Sinks
data.description:
Stock futures in the US were lower on Wednesday, with contracts on the S&P 500 futures sinking 1.3%, the Nasdaq 100 tumbling 2.1% and the Dow Jones falling about 280 points, as trade tensions continue to mount.

Shares of Nvidia sank more than 6% in premarket trading after the company revealed that the US government had blocked sales of certain AI chips to China without a license.

The company also noted it expects to take a $5.5 billion revenue hit this quarter as a result.

Traders will also monitor further corporate updates, including earnings reports from Abbott and Travelers.

On the data front, retail sales and industrial production for the US will take the spotlight and traders will also parse comments from Fed Chair Powell at the Economic Club of Chicago.

data.title: EQT's BPEA Private Equity Fund IX to Hold First Close in April
data.description:
EQT said Wednesday its BPEA Private Equity Fund IX is expected to hold its first close in April.

The Swedish private equity investor said the fund obtained commitments of more than $10 billion and is expected to achieve its target size of $12.5 billion in the summer. EQT said it expects to complete fundraising in the second half.

data.title: Mitie Group Kicks Off GBP125 Million Share Repurchase Program
data.description:
Mitie Group initiated a new share repurchase program worth up to 125 million pounds sterling on Wednesday, according to a same-day release.

The facilities management company tapped Peel Hunt to repurchase shares until March 31, 2026.

The group said 6 million of the repurchased shares will be used for its Save As You Earn scheme, which will vest in Feb. 2026, while the remaining shares will be canceled.

data.title: Bajaj Finance accepts bids for over 3-year bonds, bankers say
data.description:
India's Bajaj Finance has accepted bids worth 5 billion rupees ($58.4 million) for the sale of bonds maturing in three years and two months, three bankers said on Wednesday.

The non-banking financial company will offer a yield of 7.3763% and had invited bids from bankers and investors for the issue earlier in the day, they said.

Bajaj Finance did not respond to a Reuters email for comment.

data.title: Indonesia awards five oil and gas blocks as seeks energy security
data.description:
Indonesia awarded five oil and gas blocks to companies, its energy ministry said on Wednesday, as it attempts to replenish its oil and gas reserves and improve its energy security.

Over the coming years, the government plans to offer nearly 60 oil and gas blocks to try to reverse a decade-long decline in output.

"The government hopes these auction winners will be able to contribute to Indonesia's energy security ahead," Tri Winarno, senior official at the Energy and Mineral Resources Ministry said in an online broadcast on Wednesday's awards.

Indonesia awarded both the Gaea and Gaea II blocks in West Papua to a consortium comprising eight companies, including Enquest Petroleum Production Malaysia Ltd, BP Exploration Indonesia Ltd, CNOOC Southeast Asia Ltd.

Gaea has estimated resources of 9.6 billion barrels of oil or 71.8 trillion cubic feet of gas, while Gaea II has estimated resources of 8.5 billion barrels of oil or 35.1 tcf of gas, Tri said.

The government also awarded the Binaiya block in offshore Maluku, which has 6.7 billion barrels of oil and 15 tcf of gas potential, to state explorer PT Pertamina Hulu Energy, and Malaysia's Petronas unit PC North Mandura II Ltd and Korea's SK Earthon Co Ltd.

PC North Madura II Ltd and SK Earthon, together with Japan's INPEX Corporation, were also awarded the Sepang block offshore East Java.

The Kojo block in Makassar Strait was awarded to Armada Etan Ltd.

Once a significant oil producer and a member of the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries, Indonesia has grown increasingly reliant on imported energy.

It has offered to increase energy imports from the United States as part of negotiations on tariffs.

data.title: French Stocks End 2-Session Winning Streak
data.description:
The CAC 40 fell 0.8% to 7,279 on Wednesday, breaking a two-session winning streak, as renewed concerns over escalating trade tensions weighed on sentiment.

The US government prohibited Nvidia from selling its H20 chip to China, marking a significant escalation in the ongoing tech standoff between the US and China.

Adding to the unease, investors digested a fresh batch of corporate earnings, with ASML reporting disappointing Q1 net bookings and acknowledging it was unable to quantify the impact of the newly announced tariffs.

Meanwhile, attention is also turning to the European Central Bank's upcoming monetary policy decision on Thursday, with markets widely expecting a 25 bps rate cut.

Most stocks on the index traded lower, with Stellantis NV leading the losses, down 3.7%, followed by Edenred (-2.8%), Publicis (-2.7%), and STMicroelectronics (-2.1%).

data.title: Pound rises against dollar; UK inflation slows more than expected
data.description:
Sterling edged up against the dollar on Wednesday, as heightened uncertainty over the outlook for U.S. trade policy kept the U.S. currency under pressure across the board, while a batch of softer UK inflation data dented the pound against the euro.

British inflation slowed to an annual rate of 2.6% in March from 2.8% in February below analysts expectations of 2.7%, according to the office for National Statistics. This was below forecasts for a reading of 2.7% and the slowest pace in three months.

A measure of services inflation, something the Bank of England is monitoring closely, also eased to an annual rate of 4.7% in March, from February's 5% reading, below forecasts for 4.8%.

The pound was last up 0.3% on the day at $1.3267 , close it its highest in six months against the dollar.

It was softer on the euro, which was up 0.5% on the pound at 85.66 pence.

Price pressures will likely rise in the months ahead, as the impact from U.S. President Donald Trump's tariffs filters through to consumers.

Economists at Deutsche Bank believe this effect will not be enough to push the rate of inflation above the BoE's current forecast for a peak at 3.7% in the third quarter this year -

and therefore, should give the Monetary Policy Committee (MPC) room to cut interest rates as the economy slows.

"Inflation will take a big step up in April, pushing above 3.25% year on year, as energy and water bills lead inflation higher. The good news? We still see inflation running below the MPC’s forecast through Q2-25 – giving the MPC enough runway to cut rates through the year," Deutsche Bank chief UK economist Sanjay Raya said.

Markets show traders expect the BoE to deliver three quarter-point cuts this year, leaving rates at 3.60% by December, their lowest since January 2023.

Trump's tariff policies have sown confusion among investors this month and sparked concern that the hit to global growth will be enough to trigger a recession. The dollar has been a major casualty of the demand from investors for non-U.S. assets, falling by more than 4% against a basket of currencies since April 2, when Trump initially unveiled his proposed suite of tariffs.

data.title: Styrene monomer (SM) prices down adjust in Europe
data.description: On Tuesday, Styrene monomer prices declined in the European market.An industry source in Europe, on condition of anonymity, informed a Polymerupdate team member,

data.title: Bulgaria's Advance Terrafund buys 26.1 ha of farmland in March
data.description:
SOFIA (Bulgaria), April 16 (SeeNews) - Bulgaria's Advance Terrafund REIT [BUL:ATER] said it purchased 26.1 hectares (ha) of farmland in March.

The company also sold 12.3 ha of agricultural land last month, Advance Terrafund said in a monthly report on Tuesday.

The fund expects to book rental revenue of a little over 9 million levs ($5.2 million/4.6 million euro) in the 2024-2025 crop year, which began on October 1, compared to 9.58 million levs in the previous year. By the end of March, Advance Terrafund had collected 21.1% of the rents for the current crop year and 87.5% for the prior one.

At the end of last month, the company owned about 18,227 ha of farmland and 6.08 ha of urban land.

As of 0947 CEST on Wednesday, shares in Advance Terrafund traded 1.94% lower at 3.03 levs on the Bulgarian Stock Exchange.

(1 euro = 1.95583 levs)

data.title: ICICI Prudential's Fiscal Q4 Consolidated Profit Doubles; Shares Up 4%
data.description:
ICICI Prudential Life Insurance's consolidated profit jumped to 3.85 billion Indian rupees in the fiscal fourth quarter ended March 31 from 1.74 billion rupees a year ago.

The insurer's earnings per share climbed to 2.65 rupees from 1.21 rupees, according to a Tuesday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Net premium income in fiscal Q4 increased to 163.69 billion rupees from 147.88 billion rupees a year earlier.

The company's board recommended a final dividend of 0.85 rupees per equity share of face value of 10 rupees each for the financial year ended March 31.

Shares of the company were up nearly 4% in recent trade.

data.title: Market Chatter: Xiaomi's March Phone Actviations Highest in China
data.description:
Xiaomi's new phone activations rose 17% year over year to 3.24 million in March, the highest in mainland China, The Standard reported Tuesday, citing mainland Chinese media.

The phone maker's market share reached nearly 17.98% during the month, the report said.

Huawei's new phone activations came in second at about 20% to 3.23 million and a 17.9% market share, the report said.

Vivo was third with its new phone activations falling 2.7%, Oppo at 7.7%.

Apple was at fifth with 2.5 million new phones activated, the report said.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Italian Approval on UniCredit-Banco BPM Deal Could Come With ConditionsMarket Talk
data.description: Italy's opinion on whether to use its so-called golden powers to allow UniCredit's takeover of smaller peer Banco BPM could come earlier than expected and with conditions, Equita says in a research note after two Italian newspapers reported the decision could land in the next few days. A conditional approval is reportedly likely, with limits on branch reductions and on the rationalization of investment banking activities, guarantees on its headquarters' location and a potential exit from Russia. "These elements do not, in our opinion, diminish the industrial sense of the deal; however, it will be necessary to understand their magnitude in detail in order to properly assess the impact on the generation of synergies and value creation from the transaction," analyst Andrea Lisi writes. (elena.vardon@wsj.com)

data.title: Pen Aviation Adopts Crypto Payments with Deus X Pay
data.description:
VILNIUS, LITHUANIA / ACCESS Newswire / April 16, 2025 / Pen Aviation, a leading provider of comprehensive business aviation, Unmanned Aircraft System (UAS) solutions, and consultancy services, is now accepting cryptocurrency payments. This exciting partnership made possible through a partnership with Deus X Pay-a regulated virtual asset service provider-will enable Pen Aviation to offer its clients seamless and secure transactions using digital assets.

For Pen Aviation's elite clientele, time is invaluable, and financial transactions should be just as seamless. By integrating Deus X Pay's premium crypto payment solutions, the purchase of private jets and drone services becomes faster, more cost-effective, and completely frictionless. This strategic collaboration reflects Pen Aviation's commitment to delivering innovative, tailored solutions that cater to the evolving needs of its global clients and regional stakeholders. It also empowers both PEN UAS operators and their own end customers operating time-critical deliveries to make swift crypto payments while benefiting from cutting-edge financial technology.

Jean-Bernard Boura, Founder and Managing Director of Pen Aviation, commented on this partnership: "Business aviation and Unmanned Automated Aviation both thrive on trust, and seamless convenience. By incorporating Deus X Pay's advanced crypto payment technology, we're redefining the transaction experience and setting a new benchmark for aviation excellence across Southeast Asia. This partnership represents a bold step in our ongoing mission to stay ahead of the curve, delivering agility, innovation, and meaningful value to our discerning clientele, now and in the years to come."

This partnership also represents a key milestone in Pen Aviation's digital transformation journey. Embracing cryptocurrency payments not only enhances operational flexibility but also reinforces the company's vision to future-proof its offerings and lead the way in aviation service innovation.

Richard Crook, CEO of Deus X Pay, believes that at the heart of this partnership lies a commitment to solving real-world payment challenges, including slow processing times, high fees, and cross-border complexities. "This collaboration with Deus X Pay exemplifies Pen Aviation's commitment to providing tailored solutions that meet the evolving needs of its multinational clients and partners in the region."

Compliant crypto payment integration in the luxury asset landscape has witnessed rapid adoption due to faster settlements and reduced fees without sacrificing security - whilst meeting the needs of this unique client base. Partnering with Pen Aviation underscores the importance of offering sophisticated yet effortless payment solutions for clients, ensuring that their financial transactions are as seamless as their travel.

Deus X Pay is at the forefront of financial innovation, demonstrating that top-tier brands can adopt cryptocurrency simply opening the door to new opportunities. Together, Pen Aviation continues to deliver excellence across the aviation value chain, embracing innovation while maintaining its bespoke, client-centric approach. This latest step marks another chapter in delivering value, speed, and trust to discern clients worldwide.

About Pen Aviation

Pen Aviation Sdn. Bhd., a subsidiary of PEN GROUP SAS, provides consultancy, aircraft sales, charter, and investment services, while also designing and manufacturing certified unmanned automated systems (UAS) under its PEN UAS brand. PEN UAS delivers holistic ecosystem solutions to government and private sector clients, offering customized systems tailored to specific needs. It leverages Pen Aviation's expertise in complex automated system design and integrates DGMIND-an AI-powered platform developed by DGMIND SAS, a spin-off of Pen Aviation and also a PEN GROUP SAS subsidiary. DGMIND enhances UAS fleet operations by uniting stakeholders, integrating data, and automating management processes, driving efficiency, scalability, and sustainable business growth.

For more information, users can visit www.penaviation.com.

CONTACT

PR Manager

Tshego Tshangela

Deus X Pay

media@deusxpay.com

SOURCE: Deus X Pay

View the original press release on ACCESS Newswire

data.title: UK's Mitie gains on raising annual profit outlook, share buyback plan
data.description:
** British outsourcer Mitie Group up 4.8% to 127.8p

** Co raises annual profit outlook to forecast double-digit percentage growth in rev, driven by rise in services provided and cost-checking measures

** Launches share buyback programme of 125 mln pounds ($166 mln)

** Estimates profit for year ended March 31 to be ~230 mln pounds and rev growing ~13% y/y

** As of last close, MTO up ~10% this year

($1 = 0.7531 pounds)

FORM 8.5 (EPT/NON-RI)

PUBLIC OPENING POSITION DISCLOSURE/DEALING DISCLOSURE BY AN

EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER WITHOUT RECOGNISED INTERMEDIARY ("RI") STATUS (OR WHERE RI STATUS IS NOT APPLICABLE)

Rule 8.5 of the Takeover Code (the "Code")

1.         KEY INFORMATION

(a) Name of exempt principal trader:

(b) Name of offeror/offeree in relation to whose relevant securities this form relates:

(d) Date position held/dealing undertaken:

     For an opening position disclosure, state the latest practicable date prior to the disclosure

15/04/2025

(e) In addition to the company in 1(b) above, is the exempt principal trader making disclosures in respect of any other party to the offer?

     If it is a cash offer or possible cash offer, state "N/A"

N/A

2.         POSITIONS OF THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER

If there are positions or rights to subscribe to disclose in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 2(a) or (b) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security.

All interests and all short positions should be disclosed.

Details of any open stock-settled derivative positions (including traded options), or agreements to purchase or sell relevant securities, should be given on a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions).

(b)        Rights to subscribe for new securities (including directors' and other employee options)

  • Class of relevant security in relation to which subscription right exists:

  • Details, including nature of the rights concerned and relevant percentages:

3.         DEALINGS (IF ANY) BY THE EXEMPT PRINCIPAL TRADER

Where there have been dealings in more than one class of relevant securities of the offeror or offeree named in 1(b), copy table 3(a), (b), (c) or (d) (as appropriate) for each additional class of relevant security dealt in.

The currency of all prices and other monetary amounts should be stated.

(b)        Cash-settled derivative transactions

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. CFD

    e.g. opening/closing a long/short position, increasing/reducing a long/short position

  • Number of reference securities

  • Price per unit

(c)        Stock-settled derivative transactions (including options)

(i)         Writing, selling, purchasing or varying

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description e.g. call option

  • Writing, purchasing, selling, varying etc.

  • Number of securities to which option relates

  • Exercise price per unit

    e.g. American, European etc.

  • Expiry date

  • Option money paid/ received per unit

(ii)        Exercise

  • Class of relevant security

  • Product description

    e.g. call option

  • Exercising/ exercised against

  • Number of securities

  • Exercise price per unit

(d)        Other dealings (including subscribing for new securities)

  • Class of relevant security

  • Nature of dealing

    e.g. subscription, conversion

  • Details

  • Price per unit (if applicable)

4.         OTHER INFORMATION

(a)        Indemnity and other dealing arrangements

  • Details of any indemnity or option arrangement, or any agreement or understanding, formal or informal, relating to relevant securities which may be an inducement to deal or refrain from dealing entered into by the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any party to the offer or any person acting in concert with a party to the offer:

    Irrevocable commitments and letters of intent should not be included.  If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(b)        Agreements, arrangements or understandings relating to options or derivatives

  • Details of any agreement, arrangement or understanding, formal or informal, between the exempt principal trader making the disclosure and any other person relating to:

    (i)  the voting rights of any relevant securities under any option; or

    (ii) the voting rights or future acquisition or disposal of any relevant securities to which any derivative is referenced:

    If there are no such agreements, arrangements or understandings, state "none"

  • None

(c)        Attachments

  • Is a Supplemental Form 8 (Open Positions) attached?

  • NO

Date of disclosure:

16/04/2025

Contact name:

Umika Patel

Telephone number:

+44 (0) 0207 220 0529

Public disclosures under Rule 8 of the Code must be made to a Regulatory Information Service.

The Panel's Market Surveillance Unit is available for consultation in relation to the Code's disclosure requirements on +44 (0)20 7638 0129.

The Code can be viewed on the Panel's website at www.thetakeoverpanel.org.uk.

data.title: Heidelberg Materials Further Expands Footprint In Australia By Acquiring Midway Concrete
data.description:
Heidelberg Materials AG :

  • FURTHER EXPANDS FOOTPRINT IN AUSTRALIA BY ACQUIRING MIDWAY CONCRETE

  • ENTERED INTO A CONDITIONAL PURCHASE AGREEMENT TO ACQUIRE

  • BOTH PARTIES HAVE AGREED NOT TO DISCLOSE THE FINANCIAL TERMS OF THE TRANSACTION

  • EXPECTED TO BE COMPLETED BY MID-2025

Report on payments to governments

16 April 2025

TAURON Polska Energia S.A.

(LEI No: 259400NAPDFBOTNCRL54)

Report on payments to governments for the year ending 31 December 2024

TAURON Polska Energia S.A. ("the Company") has today published report on payments

to governments for the year ending 31 December 2024.

The document has been submitted to the Financial Conduct Authority for publication through the National Storage Mechanism, where it will shortly be available for inspection

at https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism

The Company's report on payments to governments for the year ending 31 December 2024  is now available on its website at
